chunk_summary: This section outlines triggers for investigating under-five stunting in India, emphasizing determinant-based patterns and a first-line test bundle for children with medical red flags or growth abnormalities.
learning_objectives: Understand determinant-based patterns of under-five stunting and their implications for investigation.
Identify medical red flags and triggers for further testing in stunted children.
Learn the components of a pragmatic first-line test bundle for stunting in outpatient settings.
key_facts: Stunting investigations should be triggered by pattern recognition, not solely by HAZ values.
Investigation triggers include severe short stature (< -3 SD), height deceleration, dysmorphism, chronic diarrhea, hepatosplenomegaly, and developmental regression.
A first-line test bundle includes CBC, ferritin with CRP/ESR, urinalysis, TSH with free T4, and coeliac screening (tTG-IgA and total IgA).
Stunted but otherwise well children should first receive determinant correction (diet, anemia, WASH) before broad testing.
Stool microscopy or antigen testing is recommended for chronic diarrhea or malabsorption features.
reference_quote: ‘Short stature in childhood: challenges and choices’ emphasizes the importance of accurate measurements and pattern recognition in diagnosing growth disorders. (Reference 1)
document_key_topics: First-line test bundle for stunted children
